serverNeeds.description: Public client 'Discuss' write: single-writer lifecycle fn (sent→discussing) + public POST /api/public/recommendations/:ws/:recId/discuss, plus optionally a client-authored discussion-thread write (thread is admin-write-only today). Only needed if Q2 adopts the prototype Discuss semantics.
serverNeeds.evidence: Edge defined in CLIENT_REC_TRANSITIONS (server/state-machines.ts:133-134) and counted (routes/recommendations.ts:480-499); thread table + admin-only write exist (server/rec-discussion.ts; routes :1031-1046 under requireWorkspaceAccess); recommendation-lifecycle.ts:75-213 exports send/autoSent/approve/strike/unstrike/throttle/fix — no discuss writer, no public route.
serverNeeds.whereItLives: server/recommendation-lifecycle.ts (new discussRecommendation) + server/routes/recommendations.ts public section + server/rec-discussion.ts

serverNeeds.description: Approved-card progress join: serialize linked content-request status onto the public rec projection so a persistent 'you approved this — we'll update you here' card has a real data home (N5/Q5). Today only a boolean `delivered` (rec.status==='completed') is exposed.
serverNeeds.evidence: recommendation-public-projection.ts:76-83 exposes only clientStatus + delivered + actOn; no content-request status field; rec lineage exists on the request created at act-on (routes/recommendations.ts:340-475) but is never joined back into the public read.

serverNeeds.description: Recall/unsend semantics (N3/Q6): either document recall=strike (no server change) or add a sent→recalled/unsent transition + lifecycle fn. Admin-surface owned; client only observes disappearance.
serverNeeds.evidence: strikeRecommendation exists (recommendation-lifecycle.ts:130-183) and struck recs leave the curated set via isCuratedForClient (domains/recommendations/rules.ts:255-264); CLIENT_REC_TRANSITIONS (state-machines.ts:133-134) has no back-edge from sent.

openQuestions.question: Q1 Canonical home: standalone client Recommendations page vs HEAD's 4 mounts (The Issue overview, Health action-plan slot, Overview #1 card, Inbox mirror) — which is canonical; others persist/redirect/fold?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: No new tab (D4 locks the 4-tab IA); keep The Issue as canonical, Health slot carries the legacy action plan via T1 carry-over; standalone page is a C3-era proposal.

openQuestions.question: Q2 Build the sent→discussing client write (sn-recommendations-1) or keep Discuss = advisor pre-seed? Loop footer already advertises a count nothing can increment.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep advisor pre-seed for the rebuild; ship the write as its own reviewed follow-up if wanted.

openQuestions.question: Q3 Legacy self-serve capabilities (B7-B10: status updates, dismiss, regenerate, cart purchases) — preserved or deliberately retired?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Preserve via T1: keep InsightsEngine mounted as-is inside the new shell (self-contained), reskin later.

openQuestions.question: Q4 Client-facing dollar display: confirm banded impactBand only, never raw $/mo (prototype shows raw '$3,900/mo').
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Banded only — A2 is a trust invariant; treat as non-negotiable, seek explicit ratification.

openQuestions.question: Q5 Approved-state UX: prototype's persistent 'we'll update you here' card (needs sn-recommendations-2) vs HEAD's drop-out + counts?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep HEAD drop-out for C2 zero-behavior re-skin; persistent card as C3 follow-up with the status join.

openQuestions.question: Q6 Recall semantics (admin overlap): recall = existing strike, or new unsend transition (sn-recommendations-3)?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Recall = strike; document it — no new transition.

openQuestions.question: Q7 Boundary: who owns the verdict/status headline + topRec (C5) — this surface or Client-portal overview? Both auditors must agree.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Client-portal overview owns the headline; this surface supplies topRecommendationId via the existing summary read.

openQuestions.question: Q8 Doc drift: FEATURE_AUDIT #607 still describes a per-bet 'Act on this' the code removed.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Update FEATURE_AUDIT #607 in the first rebuild PR touching this surface; no owner decision needed.

openQuestions.question: Per-flag disposition for the six gating flags (strategy-the-issue, the-issue-client-spine, the-issue-client-next-bets, the-issue-client-return-hook, client-ia-v2, strategy-competitor-send) under the rebuild's flag strategy.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: UI-shell flags map to the rebuild retirement track; strategy-the-issue and strategy-competitor-send stay backend-lifecycle-governed; enumerate the mapping in the build plan per the 2026-07-02 owner direction.

verdicts.evidence: CLIENT_REC_TRANSITIONS defines the sent→discussing edge (server/state-machines.ts:132-133); the discussing status is counted in the responses view but only ever READ, never written (server/routes/recommendations.ts:490-505 counts; server/intelligence/client-signals-slice.ts:563,584; server/routes/workspaces.ts:216). No discussRecommendation writer exists anywhere — recommendation-lifecycle.ts exports send/autoSent/approve/strike/unstrike/throttle/fix only (approve at ~120, strike at 130-183, unstrike 184-197, throttle 199-213, fix ~217); a full server-wide grep for 'discussRecommendation' and '/discuss' returns nothing. The only public client write is /act-on (routes/recommendations.ts:341-475), which via approveRecommendation writes ONLY the sent|discussing→approved edge — never sets clientStatus='discussing'. The discussion THREAD write route (routes/recommendations.ts:1038) is gated by requireWorkspaceAccess (admin HMAC), and rec-discussion.ts exposes only listRecDiscussion + addRecDiscussionEntry — no public thread-write. So both halves of the 'partial' claim hold: the discussing state edge is defined+read but has no client writer, and the thread is admin-write-only.

stripEmvFromPublicRecs (server/recommendation-public-projection.ts:36-88) is the public allow-list; when exposeClientStatus it emits only clientStatus (cfStatus), delivered = (r.status==='completed'), and actOn (lines 76-84). No content-request status field, no join to the linked content request — a grep of the projection + client-insight-recommendation-view-model.ts for contentRequest/requestStatus/deliveryStatus returns nothing. Rec→content-request lineage IS created at act-on (recommendationId stamped on the request, routes/recommendations.ts ~395-410; persisted in content-requests.ts:249,280) but is only joined back on the ADMIN side, in strategy-issue-lenses.ts:175-201 (requestByRecId + contentRequestStageOf), reachable only via GET /api/workspaces/:id/issue-lenses under requireWorkspaceAccess (admin HMAC) — never in a public route. So the 'no' verdict for a PUBLIC progress-join holds. Minor note (does not change the verdict): an admin-side join+stage-derivation helper pattern already exists to model the new public one on, but the public projection allow-list addition + view-model wiring is genuinely net-new. M effort stands (allow-list + join + serialization, no migration).

strikeRecommendation exists (server/recommendation-lifecycle.ts:130-183) and struck recs leave the curated set: isCuratedForClient returns false for lifecycle==='struck' (shared/recommendation-predicates.ts:40-42, re-exported through domains/recommendations/rules.ts:264). CLIENT_REC_TRANSITIONS has NO back-edge from sent (server/state-machines.ts:132-137: sent→[approved,declined,discussing]; approved/declined terminal). A server-wide grep for recall/unsend/recalled/unsent finds no lifecycle fn or route — the sole 'recall' hit is a doc comment on autoSent describing a UI affordance (shared/types/recommendations.ts:74), i.e. recall today is effectively strike, no dedicated writer. Both offered paths are consistent with an S effort: the 'document recall=strike' path is zero server change; the 'add sent→recalled edge' path is a small state-machine + lifecycle-fn addition. S holds.
